title: EC2 security groups should not allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to remote server administration ports
description: This control checks whether an Amazon EC2 security group allows ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to remote server administration ports (ports 22 and 3389). The control fails if the security group allows ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to port 22 or 3389. (+1 related finding(s))
what_is_wrong: This control checks whether an Amazon EC2 security group allows ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to remote server administration ports (ports 22 and 3389). The control fails if the security group allows ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to port 22 or 3389. (+1 related finding(s))
what_the_fix_does: Restricts risky inbound security-group rules on unauthorized public ports.

findings.title: Security groups should only allow unrestricted incoming traffic for authorized ports
EC2 security groups should not allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to remote server administration ports
